channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: ExPEDitions
item.description: DARPA is soliciting innovative solutions in foundational technologies to produce high-energy-density, high power-density electrically rechargeable batteries and fuel cells and rapidly transition them to end users. | See Program

item.title: Notice of Extension for RFA-TR-25-002 Preclinical Proof of Concept Studies for Rare Diseases (R21 Clinical Trial Not Allowed)
item.description: Notice NOT-TR-26-009 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of Extension of the Expiration Date "Translational Research in Maternal and Pediatric Pharmacology and Therapeutics (R21 Clinical Trial Optional)"
item.description: Notice NOT-HD-26-003 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of Extension of the Expiration Date for "Translational Research in Maternal and Pediatric Pharamacology and Therapeutics (R01 Clinical Trial Optional)"
item.description: Notice NOT-HD-26-002 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of Pre-application Webinar for the NCI Community Oncology Research Program (NCORP)
item.description: Notice NOT-CA-26-016 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: What Is the Difference Between an NIH Institute, Center, or Office’s Highlighted Topic and General Interest Area?
item.description: NIH Institutes, Centers, and Offices (ICOs) communicate their research priorities through a variety of channels. For example, general interest areas may be discussed on an ICOs’ profile page or they may be expressed through a specific Highlighted Topic. In any case, the ICO may fund research in a wide range of scientific areas within their mission, whether or not they are explicitly identified as a particular Highlighted Topic.

item.title: Podcast: AI-driven tool targets hearing loss
item.description: Yasmin Kassim has developed a deep learning-based tool that allows unparalleled insights into the hair cells that allow us to hear...

item.title: Notice of Early Expiration of PAR-25-377 Unveiling Health and Healthcare Disparities in Non-Communicable and Chronic Diseases in Latin America: Setting the Stage for Better Health Outcomes Across the Hemisphere (R01 - Clinical Trials Not Allowed)
item.description: Notice NOT-MD-26-002 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of Early Expiration of PAR-25-342 Interventions on Health and Healthcare Disparities on Non-Communicable and Chronic Diseases in Latin America: Improving Health Outcomes Across the Hemisphere (R01 - Clinical Trial Required)
item.description: Notice NOT-MD-26-001 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of Participation of the Environmental influences on Child Health Outcomes (ECHO) Program in PA-26-001 - Administrative Supplements to Existing NIH Grants and Cooperative Agreements (Parent Admin Supp Clinical Trial Optional)
item.description: Notice NOT-OD-26-049 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: NSF commits $8B to sustain U.S. scientific presence in Antarctica
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced today it awarded KBR Services LLC (KBR) the Antarctic Science and Engineering Support Contract to provide planning, management and execution support...

item.title: June 19 (Friday), 2026: NIH Closed for the Federal Holiday
item.description: NIH (including help desks) will be closed on Friday, June 19, 2026 for the federal holiday.

item.title: Clarifying Percent Effort and Support for Career Development (K) Award Recipients
item.description: For most career development (K) award programs, the K award recipient must commit at the minimum 9 person months, equivalent to 75% full-time professional effort, directly to their research project and career development activities. The recipient can devote the remaining effort (up to 25%) to additional research, teaching, clinical work, or other efforts complementary to their career development.

item.title: NIH Small Business Program Funding Opportunities Now Available – Applicant Support Webinar on June 9
item.description: NIH’s Seed Fund is back, with new Small Business program funding available and several upcoming opportunities to help applicants develop strong applications. We also encourage you join us for a live event on June 9 to hear from NIH experts and become familiar with the new opportunities and more.

item.title: Acknowledging Foreign Components throughout NIH Application and Award Processes
item.description: NIH supports international scientific collaboration when conducted transparently and in accordance with NIH requirements. The key expectation is timely and accurate disclosure of foreign components and related activities. Here we share reminders about how to properly acknowledge foreign components in NIH grant applications, progress reports, and manuscripts resulting from NIH awards.
